content.mi:
Te Hokinga Nui

Ka tere mai a Tūtarakauika ki te tai, ka pakaru tōna tinana nui i te ngaru.

Ka piki a Te Tahi ki runga i tōna tuara.

Mā te kaha o tana hiku, ka anga te tohorā nui ki Whakatāne.

He tere rawa tā rātou haere—

ka koropupū te moana i muri i a rātou

…ā, ka kitea ngā waka whero e mānu atu ana i tawhiti.
content.en:
The Great Journey Home

Tūtarakauika glided toward the shore, his massive body breaking through the waves.

Te Tahi climbed onto his back.

With a powerful sweep of his tail, the great whale turned toward Whakatāne.

They travelled fast—

so fast that the sea frothed behind them

...and the red hulls of the people’s waka could be seen drifting far in the distance.
